verdict.answer_first: A states it immediately: "ACID is a set of four guarantees"; B also answers first but adds framing before the acronym.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives the clearest bottom line: "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt"; B surveys and ends with a follow-up offer.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use standard ACID explanations and common examples.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a clean rule: "It matters most when wrong data has real cost" and "It matters less" for logs/caches.
